notes: Spec asserts: a long-idle mistral-vibe session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during the idle window, and the next user prompt resumes ready->working. Vibe is a single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> dir via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth is anchored to 'start' UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession or the real session (whichever the process scanner mints first) satisfies the birth, and the turn/idle phases anchor forward so the matcher lands the working transitions on the real session. Persistence is the daemon's adapter-agnostic PID-liveness skip: sweepStaleSnapshot (pid_manager.go:942-948) returns early when snap.pid>0 && Kill(pid,0)==nil, so the idle-past-TTL session is not deleted while the vibe process is alive; if that regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (transcript_removed for the primary session, or a flicker off ready). Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL in ~45s. observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed live meta.json config.active_model default); tokens_nonzero=true (turn_done emits stats.context_tokens); cost_nonzero=false (the adapter never reads session_cost, so per-turn cost is not emitted).

text: Session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts> id, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready -> working
Text-only assistant reply (no tool_calls) settles turn_done back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; the pid>0 liveness skip holds the bound session in ready during the idle
Next user prompt after the idle: ready -> working resumes normally on the same session
Resume turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
